title: ReportTask matches wrong block when multiple blocks share the same type

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
The `customCodeBlockRenderer` in `ReportTask.tsx` only matched blocks by `type` (the `class:` field in the YAML). When a report configuration contains two blocks of the same class (e.g. two `FeedbackAnalysis` blocks), `reportBlocks.find(b => b.type === blockClass)` always returns the first matching block, rendering the wrong block in the second position.

**Root cause:** The code fence metadata — ````block name="Feedback Analysis"``` — includes a `name` attribute that maps to `b.name` in the reportBlocks data, but this was never extracted or used for matching.

**Fix:** Extract the name from the code fence meta string via `node?.data?.meta` (react-markdown v9 exposes the raw info string here), then prefer a type+name match before falling back to type-only. Fixed in `dashboard/components/ReportTask.tsx` lines 321-330.
